The web search also returned no usable results. Both external research calls failed to return relevant material, which I'll note explicitly. I'll proceed from established biophysical principles and local evidence.

**Research summary**: Neither the ML paper index (`research_papers` snippet_search) nor `web_search` returned usable amyloid-kinetics content. The structural justification therefore rests on the well-established Knowles/Cohen/Oosawa nucleation-and-growth framework, which I already know: seeded aggregation curves are sigmoidal in time, and the dominant, robust mechanistic effect of adding seed is to **shorten the lag/half-time** — i.e. translate the sigmoid leftward along the time axis — rather than to rescale the plateau amplitude. The integrated rate laws have half-times that depend on monomer and seed concentration, and these scalings are most naturally captured as a horizontal shift of the inflection point.

This is exactly what my proposed logistic-with-shifted-midpoint
